violations.description: Hot storage of potentially hazardous food. — --Hot food storage facilities/equipment was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining temperature of potentially hazardous food at 140F or greater. -Warmer holding chicken and ribs had an ambient temp of 127.6F and a rotisserie chicken temperature of 133.7F

Reheating food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not reheated rapidly so all parts reached a temperature of 165F for 15 seconds within one hour. -At the time of inspection the employee stated their rotisserie chicken was taken out every night, cooled in the walk-in, then reheated each morning in the warmer. Warmer ambient temperature was sitting at 127F. This would be insufficient to bring food up to 165F reheat.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Repackaged potatoe/crab salads dated 3.5 weeks out. -Ready-to-eat hot holding items (rotisserie chicken and ribs) in the warmer are dated may 3rd. Today is April 23rd
Variance requirement for specialized processing methods. — --Food establishment did not obtain approved variance or HACCP plan from regulatory authority for special process of.
